task_completion.description: The browser has navigated to the correct Jitsi Meet room URL.
The agent has successfully joined the meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Jitsi Meet interface is visible (e.g., meeting toolbar at the bottom, video tiles), indicating the agent has passed the pre-join screen.

task_completion.description: The display name is set to 'Coach Martinez'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'Coach Martinez' is visible on the local user's video thumbnail, in the participant list, or on the pre-join screen before entering.

task_completion.description: The video quality settings panel is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or panel (typically titled 'Performance settings' or 'Video quality') is open and overlaying the main meeting interface in the final screenshots.

task_completion.description: The video quality is set to 'Low definition'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the open quality settings panel, the slider or selection indicator is explicitly set to the 'Low definition' option.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser (e.g., Chromium, Firefox) is visible in the screenshots, rather than terminal commands like curl or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Jitsi Meet graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual Jitsi Meet web interface, not a text editor, local HTML file, or manipulated mock UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element pane) are not open and being used to alter the text of the quality labels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Jitsi Meet instance is hosted locally at http://localhost:8080. The target room URL is http://localhost:8080/VirtualFitnessQ4. The target display name is 'Coach Martinez'. The video quality settings in Jitsi Meet use a slider with four specific labels: 'Audio only', 'Low definition', 'Standard definition', and 'High definition'. The agent must select 'Low definition'.
